Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: Open Circuit Design (or Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder;  take your pick).

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: Pushed an update today.  I managed to squeeze some space out of it, but not as much as I originally thought I could;  ultimately I recovered about 8um which is not terribly useful for having spent a full day and a half doing it.  The final layout area is 301.3um x 321.89um.  The fact that the height is now less than a TT slot height is purely coincidental, and probably meaningless.  All digital pinouts are on the bottom side and there is only enough space in the TT slot to get maybe 9 routes in from the side, whereas the block has 36 digital I/O.  It could be rotated 90 degrees but then the I/O are not in the preferred direction.

Author: mithro_
Content: @Tim Edwards - How does that compare to the 5V SRAM?

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: Area-wise, it's slightly less than 1/2 the area.

Author: 246tnt
Content: @Tim Edwards Well it would be easy enough to make a thin wrapper that brings the traces to the preferred routing direction ( and possibly adapts power rails so they connect nicely too ).

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content:
I thought about that but didn't do a feasibility study.  : )  Yet.
Also:  It's pretty easy to make the smaller SRAM versions, since GF used the same control block exactly, just removed a number of rows and grounded the upper address bits.  The 256 byte SRAM is 301.3um x 224.93um.  It took me less than 30 minutes to create the 256 byte layout from the 512 byte layout, but there are a couple of LVS errors I need to fix.  I should be able to get that pushed before the end of the weekend.

Author: mithro_
Content: :joke: Shouldn't it be 1/4th the size? 😛

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: The transistors scale to 1/4 area;  everything else, not so much.

Author: mithro_
Content: Yeah, sadly I knew that

Content: @urish Well not really because there aren't power gates and I'd like to run comparison between 3.3v and 5v which means everything on there must support 5V without destroying the chip ...

Author: urish

Content: (from https://gf180mcu-pdk.readthedocs.io/en/latest/analog/spice/elec_specs/elec_specs_1.html )
